Year 300 After the Conquest (300 AC) is a year in the Westerosi calendar, the 300th after Aegon I Targaryen's crowning in Oldtown which maesters and educated people use as a reference to date the years.[N 1]

300 AC starts with the wedding and death of King Joffrey I Baratheon, which takes place on the first day of the year. His brother Tommen is crowned shortly thereafter and is married to the twice-widowed Margaery Tyrell, preserving the alliance between houses Lannister and Tyrell which ensures the stability of the realm. However, the murders of Tywin and Kevan Lannister, the rise of the Faith Militant, and the invasion of Aegon Targaryen leave Westeros in turmoil.

The final chapters of A Storm of Swords, most chapters of A Feast For Crows[N 2] and all chapters of A Dance with Dragons take place in 300 AC.

Events[ ]

Conflicts

  • The War of the Five Kings appears to end, with Renly Baratheon, Balon Greyjoy, and Robb Stark dead, and Stannis Baratheon fleeing to the north.
  • Castle Black is assaulted by wildlings lead by Mance Rayder, but Stannis Baratheon arrives and defeats them with his army.
  • Euron Greyjoy leads ironborn in the taking of the Shields.
  • The last two seats that Stannis Baratheon holds in the south are being attacked by royal forces:
    • Storm's End is put under siege by the royalist forces led by Mace Tyrell.
    • Dragonstone is assaulted by the royalist forces led by Loras Tyrell, who is allegedly nearly killed in the attack.
  • The Golden Company lands in the stormlands.
  • Moat Cailin is captured by House Bolton.
  • Stannis Baratheon takes Deepwood Motte.
  • Astapor falls in a siege to the Yunkai'i.
  • With Daenerys gone, Yunkai begins the second siege of Meereen.
  • Tytos Blackwood surrenders to Jaime Lannister, ending the siege of Raventree.
  • Stannis Baratheon's army marches on Winterfell.

Other Events

  • Winter engulfs Westeros meaning there is no chance now of a last harvest.
  • The Faith Militant is reborn again. The order becomes powerful quickly.
  • A kingsmoot on Old Wyk is the first to be called on the Iron Islands in over two thousand years.
  • In response to the Iron Throne's refusal to pay back the money owed, the Iron Bank of Braavos refuses any new loans to Westeros causing economic chaos.
  • A mêlée at Runestone is held by Yohn Royce.
  • Margaery Tyrell and Cersei Lannister are held prisoner by the Faith and accused of adultery and treason.
  • Mance Rayder is held captive by House Bolton.
  • Theon Greyjoy and Jeyne Poole (the false Arya Stark) escape Winterfell.
  • Ramsay Bolton sends Jon Snow a letter claiming Stannis is dead.
  • Jon Snow is stabbed multiple times by his own men (unknown if he survived).

Births[ ]

  • The as of yet unnamed son of Mance Rayder and Dalla
  • Tyrion Tanner, bastard son of Lollys Stokeworth and an unknown rapist

Deaths[ ]

In the north

  • Dagon Codd, slain at Moat Cailin
  • Hagen, slain during the fight by Deepwood Motte
  • Bryen Farring, freezes during the march on Winterfell
  • Harwood Fell, dies from a fever
  • Frenya, possibly killed and skinned at Winterfell by Ramsay Bolton
  • Aenys Frey, killed by a trap set by Mors Umber
  • Jared Frey, possibly baked in a baked in a pie
  • Rhaegar Frey, possibly baked in a pie
  • Symond Frey, possibly baked in a pie
  • Little Walder Frey, killed at Winterfell
  • Hagen, slain during the fight by Deepwood Motte
  • Holly, possibly killed and skinned at Winterfell by Ramsay Bolton
  • Adrack Humble, slain at Moat Cailin
  • Ralf Kenning, slain at Moat Cailin
  • Kyra, who was hunted by Ramsay Bolton outside Winterfell
  • Luton, slain by Ramsay Bolton at Winterfell
  • Myrtle, possibly killed and skinned at Winterfell by Ramsay Bolton
  • Rolfe the Dwarf, slain during the fight by Deepwood Motte
  • Rowan, possibly killed and skinned at Winterfell by Ramsay Bolton
  • Squirrel, possibly killed and skinned at Winterfell by Ramsay Bolton
  • Willow Witch-eye, possibly killed and skinned at Winterfell by Ramsay Bolton
  • Yellow Dick, slain by Rowan at Winterfell

At the Wall and Beyond

  • Alan of Rosby, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Black Jack Bulwer, slain beyond the Wall by the Weeper
  • Clubfoot Karl, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Dalla, while giving birth to Mance Rayder's son
  • Dirk, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Dormund, slain during the battle of the Wall by Ser Richard Horpe
  • Garth of Greenaway, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Garth Greyfeather, slain beyond the Wall by the Weeper
  • Grubbs, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Hairy Hal, slain beyond the Wall by the Weeper
  • Harma Dogshead, slain during the battle beneath the Wall
  • Mag Mar Tun Doh Weg, slain by Donal Noye
  • Mawney, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Muttering Bill, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Donal Noye, slain while fighting Mag Mar Tun Doh Weg
  • Ollo Lophand, mutineer slain by Coldhands
  • Orphan Oss, possibly among the five mutineers slain by Coldhands
  • Patrek of King's Mountain, killed by the giant Wun Weg Wun Dar Wun
  • Red Alyn of the Rosewood, slain during the battle of the Wall
  • Rattleshirt, burned to death while appearing as Mance Rayder
  • Janos Slynt, beheaded by Jon Snow for refusing to obey orders
  • Thistle, slain beyond the Wall
  • Torwynd, died of the cold beyond the Wall
  • Varamyr, slain beyond the Wall

In King's Landing

  • Joffrey I Baratheon, poisoned during his wedding feast
  • Gregor Clegane, died from thickened manticore venom
  • Hamish the Harper, while a prisoner in Qyburn's care
  • High Septon, killed by Osney Kettleblack
  • Dontos Hollard, killed on the orders of Petyr Baelish
  • Kevan Lannister, killed with a crossbow bolt by Varys
  • Tywin Lannister, slain by his son, Tyrion Lannister, on his privy with a crossbow
  • Oberyn Martell, slain in a trial by combat against Gregor Clegane
  • Pycelle, killed by Varys
  • Gyles Rosby, of his cough
  • Senelle, from Qyburn's experiments
  • Shae, strangled by Tyrion Lannister
  • Falyse Stokeworth, from Qyburn's experiments

In the crownlands

  • Balman Byrch, slain by Bronn at Stokeworth
  • Dick Crabb, slain by Shagwell at the Whispers
  • Alester Florent, burned to death by Melisandre at Dragonstone
  • Pyg, slain by Brienne of Tarth at the Whispers
  • Shagwell, slain by Brienne at the Whispers
  • Tanda Stokeworth, dies at Stokeworth after breaking her hip
  • Timeon, slain by Brienne at the Whispers

In the riverlands

  • Biter, slain by Gendry
  • Robert Blackwood, of sickness
  • Merrett Frey, hanged by the brotherhood without banners
  • Petyr Frey, hanged by the brotherhood without banners
  • Ryman Frey, hanged by the brotherhood without banners
  • Polliver, slain by Sandor Clegane at the crossroads inn
  • Rorge, slain by Brienne of Tarth
  • Tickler, slain by Arya Stark at the crossroads inn
  • Shella Whent, the last of House Whent

In the Vale of Arryn

  • Lysa Arryn, pushed to her death by her new husband, Petyr Baelish, at the Eyrie
  • Eon Hunter, allegedly murdered by his youngest son Harlan
  • Marillion, jumped from his sky cell at the Eyrie

In the Reach

  • Pate, killed by poison in Oldtown
  • Talbert Serry, killed by Victarion Greyjoy during the taking of the Shields

In the Iron Islands

  • Baelor Blacktyde, killed by Euron Greyjoy on Old Wyk
  • Cragorn, from sounding the dragon horn at the kingsmoot on Old Wyk

In Dorne

  • Arys Oakheart, beheaded by Areo Hotah after a suicide charge by the Greenblood

Across the narrow sea

  • Barsena Blackhair, killed in the fighting pits by a boar
  • Black Fist, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Cetherys, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Cleon, slain by his own man who became Cleon the Second
  • Cleon the Second, slain by King Cutthroat
  • Dareon, killed by Cat of the Canals for being a deserter of the Night's Watch
  • Duran, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Eladon Goldenhair, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Groleo, killed by Bloodbeard
  • Harghaz, slain by Drogon
  • Hazzea, claimed to have been killed by Drogon
  • Kedry, slain by corsairs
  • Kerwin, killed on the command of Victarion Greyjoy
  • Khrazz, slain by Ser Barristan Selmy
  • King Cutthroat, slain during the siege of Astapor
  • Loyal Spear, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Quentyn Martell, killed by the dragon Rhaegal
  • Mossador, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Nurse, killed by the pale mare
  • Oppo, a dwarf murdered by sailors who falsely believed him to be Tyrion Lannister
  • Ordello, poisoned
  • Yezzan zo Qaggaz, killed by the pale mare
  • Queen Whore, slain during the siege of Astapor
  • Rafford, killed by Mercy
  • Rylona Rhee, a harpist slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Stalwart Shield, slain by the Sons of the Harpy
  • Sweets, killed by the pale mare
  • Aemon Targaryen, due to a chill and old age
  • Willam Wells, slain by corsairs
  • Mollono Yos Dob, died from the pale mare
  • Cletus Yronwood, slain by corsairs
  • Yurkhaz zo Yunzak, trampled to death by his own fleeing slaves

Marriages[ ]

  • Joffrey Baratheon and Margaery Tyrell (the so-called Purple Wedding)
  • The wedding of Petyr Baelish and Lysa Arryn
  • Bronn of the Blackwater and Lollys Stokeworth
  • The wedding of Tommen Baratheon and Margaery Tyrell, after Joffrey's death
  • Asha Greyjoy and Erik Ironmaker; Asha was not present for this wedding which was arranged by Euron Greyjoy
  • The wedding of Lyonel Corbray and a merchant's daughter
  • The wedding of Ramsay Bolton and the fake Arya Stark (Jeyne Poole)
  • The wedding of Daenerys Targaryen and Hizdahr zo Loraq
  • The wedding of Alys Karstark and Sigorn, the Magnar of Thenn
  • Axell Florent to Gerrick Kingsblood's eldest daughter
  • Brus Buckler to Gerrick Kingsblood's second daughter
  • Malegorn of Redpool to Gerrick Kingsblood's youngest daughter

Titles and Positions[ ]

King

  • Tommen Baratheon, the first of his name, is crowned King on the Iron Throne
  • Euron Greyjoy is crowned the new Iron King
  • Cleon is crowned King of Astapor
  • Cleon the Second crowns himself King of Astapor after killing Cleon
  • King Cutthroat crowns himself King of Astapor after killing Cleon the Second
  • Hizdahr zo Loraq is crowned King of Meereen

Queen

  • Margaery Tyrell is crowned (for the second time)
  • Queen Whore declares herself Queen of Astapor
Small Council

Cersei Lannister names a new small council, replacing nearly all of the old members

  • Harys Swyft is named Hand of the King to Tommen I Baratheon
  • Gyles Rosby is named master of coin
  • Orton Merryweather is named master of laws
  • Aurane Waters is named master of ships
  • Qyburn is named new master of whisperers
  • Harys Swyft is demoted to master of coin after Gyles Rosby's death
  • Orton Merryweather is named the new Hand of the King, he later resigns

After Cersei's imprisonment, Kevan Lannister is made Regent to King Tommen I. He names a new small council

  • Mace Tyrell is named Hand of the King
  • Randyll Tarly is named master of laws
  • Paxter Redwyne is named master of ships
  • Doran Martell sends Nymeria Sand to take the vacant Dornish seat

Lord

  • Rolph Spicer is made Lord of Castamere by Tywin Lannister
  • Emmon Frey is made the new Lord of Riverrun
  • Gilwood Hunter becomes the new Lord of Longbow Hall and head of House Hunter
  • Nute the Barber is made new Lord of Oakenshield by Euron Greyjoy
  • Harras Harlaw is made new Lord of Greyshield by Euron
  • Andrik the Unsmiling is made new Lord of Southshield by Euron
  • Maron Volmark is made new Lord of Greenshield by Euron

Kingsguard

  • Robert Strong is named to the Kingsguard
  • Rolly Duckfield is named a knight of the new Kingsguard of Aegon Targaryen

Knighted

  • Harrold Hardyng is knighted by Yohn Royce
  • Mychel Redfort

Castellan

  • Damion Lannister is named castellan of Casterly Rock
  • Polliver is named castellan of Harrenhal by Gregor Clegane, but is eventually slain
  • Bonifer Hasty is named castellan of Harrenhal

Other Titles

  • Jon Snow is named Lord Commander of the Night's Watch
  • Roose Bolton is made Warden of the North by Tywin Lannister
  • Daven Lannister is made Warden of the West
  • A new High Septon is named and becomes known as the High Sparrow
  • Elections are held in Volantis for the Triarchs
  • Jon Connington is titled Hand of the King to Aegon Targaryen
  • Barristan Selmy is named to Daenerys Targaryen's Queensguard and eventually assumes the title of Queen's Hand
  • Skahaz mo Kandaq is made commander of the Brazen Beasts
  • Marghaz zo Loraq is made new commander of the Brazen Beasts by King Hizdahr zo Loraq

Notes[ ]

  1. ↑ In universe, 300 AC marks the beginning of the fourth century since the foundation of the Aegon's Conquest.[1][2][3] Mathematically, however, the new century should begin in 301 AC.
  2. ↑ The first ironborn chapter, The Prophet, and possibly more, do not take place in 300 AC.
